Explore the cuTile Python compiler passes at /home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/nvidia-cutile-python/src/cuda/tile/_passes/ . I need a concise architectural summary (medium breadth).

Read and summarize each pass module:
- _passes/ast2hir.py (Python AST -> High-level IR; get_function_hir)
- _passes/hir2ir.py (HIR -> Tile IR; hir2ir function)
- _passes/dce.py (dead code elimination)
- _passes/code_motion.py (hoist_loop_invariants)
- _passes/dataflow_analysis.py (dataflow_analysis)
- _passes/propagate_divby.py (add_divby_pass - divisibility propagation)
- _passes/token_order.py (token_order_pass)
- _passes/rewrite_patterns.py (rewrite_patterns)
- _passes/loop_split.py (split_loops)
- _passes/unhoist_partition_views.py
- _passes/eliminate_assign_ops.py
- _passes/check_dtype_support.py
- _passes/ast_util.py

For each pass report:
1. Its main entry function name and file:line.
2. What transformation it performs (1-2 sentences).
3. Why it matters / what it optimizes.

Also report how the passes are orchestrated (look at the order they're called in _transform_ir in _compile.py which I've already read: eliminate_assign_ops -> dce -> dataflow_analysis -> add_divby -> token_order -> rewrite_patterns -> hoist_loop_invariants -> unhoist_partition_views -> split_loops -> dce).

Give actual function/class names with file:line references. Keep it under 600 words, structured.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

27	from .._ir.scope import Scope, LocalScope, IntMap

37	
38	def hir2ir(func_hir: hir.Function,
39	           param_aggregate_vars: Sequence[ir.Var],
40	           ir_ctx: IRContext):
41	    # Run as a coroutine using a software stack, so that we don't exceed Python's recursion limit.
42	    run_coroutine(_hir2ir_coroutine(func_hir, param_aggregate_vars, ir_ctx))
43	
44	
45	async def _hir2ir_coroutine(
46	    func_hir: hir.Function, param_aggregate_vars: Sequence[ir.Var], ir_ctx: IRContext
47	):
48	    scope = _create_scope(func_hir, ir_ctx, call_site=None, parent_scopes=(),
49	                          concrete_func_desc=func_hir.desc)
50	    for local_idx, var in zip(func_hir.param_local_indices, param_aggregate_vars, strict=True):
51	        scope.local[local_idx] = var
52	
53	    ir_builder = ir.Builder.get_current()
54	    with scope.make_current():
55	        try:
56	            await _dispatch_hir_block_inner(func_hir.body, ir_builder)
57	        except Exception as e:
58	            if ir_ctx.log_ir_on_error:
59	                highlight_loc = e.loc if hasattr(e, 'loc') else None
60	                ir_str = "\n".join(op.to_string(highlight_loc=highlight_loc)
61	                                   for op in ir_builder.ops)
62	                print(f"==== Partial cuTile IR ====\n\n{ir_str}\n\n", file=sys.stderr)
63	            raise
64	
65	
66	def _create_scope(func_hir: hir.Function, ir_ctx: IRContext, call_site: Loc | None,
67	                  parent_scopes: tuple[LocalScope, ...],
68	                  concrete_func_desc: FunctionDesc) -> Scope:
69	    local_scope = LocalScope(func_hir.local_names, ir_ctx)
70	    return Scope(parent_scopes + (local_scope,), None, None, call_site, IntMap(), func_hir,
71	                 concrete_func_desc=concrete_func_desc)
72	
73	
74	def _concretize_func_desc(func_hir: hir.Function, ir_ctx: IRContext) -> FunctionDesc:
75	    # Mint a fresh FunctionDesc whose `specialization_id` makes its synthesized
76	    # linkage name unique across every inlining.
77	    return dataclasses.replace(func_hir.desc,
78	                               specialization_id=ir_ctx.next_function_specialization_id())
79	
80	
81	def retarget_loc(loc: Loc, scope: Scope) -> Loc:
82	    if loc.is_unknown():
83	        return loc
84	
85	    # Splice in the scope's call site and, if this loc belongs to the function
86	    # currently being inlined, swap in the per-specialization FunctionDesc so
87	    # emitted ops carry the right debug info.
88	    new_function = loc.function
89	    if loc.function is scope.func_hir.desc:
90	        new_function = scope.concrete_func_desc
91	    return dataclasses.replace(loc, function=new_function, call_site=scope.call_site)
92	
93	
94	async def dispatch_hir_block(block: hir.Block, cur_builder: ir.Builder | None = None):
95	    if cur_builder is None:
96	        cur_builder = ir.Builder.get_current()
97	    await _dispatch_hir_block_inner(block, cur_builder)
98	
99	
100	async def _dispatch_hir_block_inner(block: hir.Block, builder: ir.Builder):
101	    cursor = 0  # Pre-initialize to guarantee it's defined in the `except` block
102	    try:
103	        scope = Scope.get_current()
104	        for cursor, call in enumerate(block.calls):
105	            loc = retarget_loc(call.loc, scope)
106	            with _wrap_exceptions(loc), builder.change_loc(loc):
107	                await _dispatch_call(call, scope)
108	            if builder.is_terminated:
109	                # The current block has been terminated, e.g. by flattening an if-else
110	                # with a constant condition (`if True: break`).
111	                return
112	        cursor = len(block.calls)
113	
114	        loc = retarget_loc(block.jump_loc, scope)
115	        with _wrap_exceptions(loc), builder.change_loc(loc):
116	            _dispatch_hir_jump(block, scope)
117	    except Exception:
118	        if builder.ir_ctx.log_ir_on_error:
119	            hir_params = ", ".join(str(p) for p in block.params)
120	            hir_lines = [str(c) for c in block.calls]
121	            if block.jump is not None:
122	                hir_lines.append(block.jump_str())
123	            hir_str = "\n".join("{}{}".format("--> " if i == cursor else "    ", c)
124	                                for i, c in enumerate(hir_lines))
125	            print(f"==== HIR for ^{block.block_id}({hir_params}) ====\n{hir_str}\n",
126	                  file=sys.stderr)
127	        raise
128	
129	
130	def _dispatch_hir_jump(block: hir.Block, scope: Scope):
131	    match block.jump:
132	        case hir.Jump.END_BRANCH:
133	            end_branch(_resolve_operand(block.result, scope) if block.have_result else None)
134	        case hir.Jump.CONTINUE:
135	            assert not block.have_result
136	            continue_()
137	        case hir.Jump.BREAK:
138	            assert not block.have_result
139	            break_()
140	        case hir.Jump.RETURN:
141	            return_(_resolve_operand(block.result, scope) if block.have_result else None)
142	        case None: pass
143	        case _: assert False
144	
145	
146	@contextmanager
147	def _wrap_exceptions(loc: Loc):
148	    with loc:
149	        try:
150	            yield
151	        except TileError:
152	            raise
153	        except Exception as e:
154	            raise TileInternalError(str(e)) from e
155	
156	
157	async def _dispatch_call(hir_call: hir.Call, scope: Scope):
158	    callee_var = _resolve_operand(hir_call.callee, scope)
159	    args = []
160	    for x in hir_call.args:
161	        if isinstance(x, hir.Starred):
162	            tup_var = _resolve_operand(x.value, scope)
163	            assert isinstance(tup_var, Var)
164	            tup_ty = tup_var.get_type()
165	            if not isinstance(tup_ty, TupleTy):
166	                raise TileTypeError(f"Expected a tuple after *, got {tup_ty}")
167	            tup_value = tup_var.get_aggregate()
168	            assert isinstance(tup_value, TupleValue)
169	            args.extend(tup_value.items)
170	        else:
171	            args.append(_resolve_operand(x, scope))
172	    kwargs = {}
173	    for k, v in hir_call.kwargs:
174	        resolved_val = _resolve_operand(v, scope)
175	        if k is None:
176	            assert isinstance(resolved_val, Var)
177	            dict_ty = resolved_val.get_type()
178	            if not isinstance(dict_ty, DictTy):
179	                raise TileTypeError(f"Expected a dictionary after **, got {dict_ty}")
180	            dict_value = resolved_val.get_aggregate()
181	            assert isinstance(dict_value, DictValue)
182	            for item_key, item_value in zip(dict_ty.keys, dict_value.values, strict=True):
183	                kwargs[item_key] = item_value
184	        else:
185	            kwargs[k] = resolved_val
186	    retval = await call(callee_var, args, kwargs)
187	    if hir_call.result is not None and retval is not None:
188	        scope.hir2ir_varmap[hir_call.result.id] = retval
189	
190	
191	async def _call_user_defined(callee_hir: hir.Function,
192	                             arg_list: list[Var | tuple[Var, ...]],
193	                             builder: ir.Builder,
194	                             parent_scopes: tuple[LocalScope, ...] = ()):
195	    _check_recursive_call(builder.loc)
196	
197	    # Activate a fresh Scope. Each inlining gets its own concretized
198	    # FunctionDesc so that DI never merges two specializations whose generated
199	    # IR might differ.
200	    new_scope = _create_scope(callee_hir, builder.ir_ctx, call_site=builder.loc,
201	                              parent_scopes=parent_scopes,
202	                              concrete_func_desc=_concretize_func_desc(callee_hir,
203	                                                                       builder.ir_ctx))
204	    with new_scope.make_current():
205	        # Call store_var() to bind arguments to parameters.
206	        for arg, local_idx, param_loc in zip(arg_list, callee_hir.param_local_indices,
207	                                             callee_hir.param_locs, strict=True):
208	            if isinstance(arg, tuple):
209	                # Handle the *vararg parameter
210	                arg = build_tuple(arg)
211	            elif isinstance(arg, dict):
212	                arg = build_dict(tuple(arg.keys()), tuple(arg.values()))
213	            store_var(local_idx, arg, param_loc)
214	
215	        # Dispatch the function body. Use resume_after() to break the call stack
216	        # and make sure we stay within the Python's recursion limit.
217	        await resume_after(dispatch_hir_block(callee_hir.body, builder))
218	
219	    assert callee_hir.body.have_result
220	    ret = _process_return_value(
221	            new_scope.hir2ir_varmap[callee_hir.body.result.id], new_scope.local, builder)
222	    new_scope.local.mark_dead()
223	    return ret
224	
225	
226	async def call_function(callee: Callable, *args: Var, **kwargs: Var):
227	    return await _call_function(callee, args, kwargs, ir.Builder.get_current())
228	
229	
230	async def _call_function(callee: Callable,
231	                         args: Sequence[Var],
232	                         kwargs: Mapping[str, Var],
233	                         builder: ir.Builder):
234	    sig = get_signature(callee)
235	    arg_list = _bind_args(sig, callee.__name__, args, kwargs)
236	    if is_stub(callee) or is_supported_builtin_func(callee):
237	        return await _call_builtin(callee, arg_list, builder)
238	    else:
239	        callee_hir = get_function_hir(callee, entry_point=False)
240	        return await _call_user_defined(callee_hir, arg_list, builder)
241	
242	
243	async def _call_builtin(callee, arg_list: list[Var | tuple[Var, ...]], builder: ir.Builder):
244	    impl_registry = ImplRegistry.get_current()
245	    try:
246	        impl = impl_registry.op_implementations[callee]
247	    except KeyError:
248	        impl = getattr(callee, "_cutile_custom_implementation_handler", None)
249	        if impl is None:
250	            raise NotImplementedError(f"Missing implementation for {callee}")
251	        arg_list = [callee, *arg_list]
252	
253	    result = impl(*arg_list)
254	    if impl._is_coroutine:
255	        result = await result
256	
257	    if builder.is_terminated:
258	        # The current block has been terminated, e.g. by flattening an if-else
259	        # with a constant condition (`if True: break`). Ignore the `result` in this case.
260	        return None
261	
262	    # Map the result variable
263	    if result is None:
264	        result = loosely_typed_const(None)
265	    assert isinstance(result, Var)
266	    return result
267	
268	
269	_DTYPE_CONSTRUCTOR_SIGNATURE = inspect.signature(lambda x=0, /: None)
270	_POINTER_INFO_SIGNATURE = inspect.signature(PointerInfo)
271	
272	
273	async def call(callee_var: Var, args, kwargs) -> Var | None:
274	    builder = ir.Builder.get_current()
275	    callee_ty = callee_var.get_type()
276	    if isinstance(callee_ty, FunctionTy):
277	        return await _call_function(callee_ty.func, args, kwargs, builder)
278	    elif isinstance(callee_ty, BoundMethodTy):
279	        bound_method = callee_var.get_aggregate()
280	        assert isinstance(bound_method, BoundMethodValue)
281	        return await _call_function(callee_ty.func, (bound_method.bound_self, *args), kwargs,
282	                                    builder)
283	    elif isinstance(callee_ty, DTypeConstructor):
284	        arg_list = _bind_args(_DTYPE_CONSTRUCTOR_SIGNATURE, callee_ty.dtype.name, args, kwargs)
285	        [x] = arg_list
286	        return dtype_constructor(callee_ty.dtype, x)
287	    elif isinstance(callee_ty, ClosureTy):
288	        func_name = callee_ty.func_hir.desc.name
289	        if func_name is None:
290	            func_name = callee_ty.func_hir.desc.short_str()
291	        arg_list = _bind_args(callee_ty.func_hir.signature, func_name, args, kwargs,
292	                              callee_var.get_aggregate().default_values)
293	        parent_scopes = _get_closure_parent_scopes(callee_ty, callee_var.get_aggregate(),
294	                                                   builder.ir_ctx)
295	        return await _call_user_defined(callee_ty.func_hir, arg_list, builder, parent_scopes)
296	    elif isinstance(callee_ty, TypeTy) and dataclasses.is_dataclass(callee_ty.ty):
297	        dataclass_info = get_dataclass_info(callee_ty.ty)
298	        param_names = tuple(dataclass_info.init_signature.parameters)
299	        # Add an extra `None` to args for the `self` parameter
300	        arg_list = _bind_args(dataclass_info.init_signature, callee_ty.ty.__name__,
301	                              (None, *args), kwargs)
302	        assert len(dataclass_info.field_names) + 1 == len(arg_list)
303	        items = tuple(arg_list[param_names.index(name)] for name in dataclass_info.field_names)
304	        return build_dataclass_instance(items, dataclass_info)
305	    elif isinstance(callee_ty, TypeTy) and callee_ty.ty is PointerInfo:
306	        arg_list = _bind_args(_POINTER_INFO_SIGNATURE, "PointerInfo", args, kwargs)
307	        return await _call_builtin(PointerInfo, arg_list, builder)
308	    elif isinstance(callee_ty, TypeTy) and issubclass(callee_ty.ty, Enum):
309	        if len(args) != 1 or kwargs:
310	            raise TileTypeError("Enum constructor takes exactly one positional argument")
311	        arg = args[0]
312	        if not arg.is_constant():
313	            raise TileTypeError("Enum constructor argument must be a constant")
314	        val = arg.get_constant()
315	        try:
316	            return loosely_typed_const(callee_ty.ty(val))
317	        except ValueError:
318	            raise TileValueError(f"{val!r} is not a valid {callee_ty.ty.__name__}")
319	    else:
320	        raise TileTypeError(f"Cannot call an object of type {callee_ty}")
321	
322	
323	def _get_closure_parent_scopes(ty: ClosureTy, val: ClosureValue,
324	                               ir_ctx: IRContext) -> tuple[LocalScope, ...]:
325	    ret: list[LocalScope | None] = [None for _ in ty.frozen_capture_types_by_depth]
326	    for live_scope in ty.captured_scopes:
327	        ret[live_scope.depth] = live_scope.local_scope
328	
329	    for depth, (func, frozen_local_indices, frozen_vars) in enumerate(
330	                zip(ty.func_hir.enclosing_funcs,
331	                    ty.func_hir.captures_by_depth,
332	                    val.frozen_captures_by_depth,
333	                    strict=True)):
334	        # Scope at this depth is either live or frozen (mutually exclusive)
335	        assert (frozen_vars is None) != (ret[depth] is None)
336	        if frozen_vars is not None:
337	            ret[depth] = LocalScope.create_frozen(func.local_names, frozen_local_indices,
338	                                                  frozen_vars, ir_ctx)
339	    return tuple(ret)
340	
341	
342	def _process_return_value(retval: Var, callee_scope: LocalScope, builder: ir.Builder) -> Var:
343	    ty = retval.get_type_allow_invalid()
344	    if not ty.is_aggregate():
345	        return retval
346	
347	    if isinstance(ty, ClosureTy):
348	        retval = _freeze_returned_closure(retval, callee_scope, builder)
349	        ty = retval.get_type()
350	
351	    old_items = retval.get_aggregate().as_tuple()
352	    new_items = tuple(_process_return_value(x, callee_scope, builder) for x in old_items)
353	    if any(old is not new for old, new in zip(old_items, new_items, strict=True)):
354	        new_agg_val = ty.make_aggregate_value(new_items)
355	        retval = builder.make_aggregate(new_agg_val, ty)
356	
357	    return retval
358	
359	
360	def _freeze_returned_closure(retval: Var, callee_scope: LocalScope, builder: ir.Builder) -> Var:
361	    ty = retval.get_type_allow_invalid()
362	    assert isinstance(ty, ClosureTy)
363	
364	    if len(ty.captured_scopes) == 0 or ty.captured_scopes[-1].local_scope is not callee_scope:
365	        # For example:
366	        #
367	        #    def kernel():
368	        #        def f1():
369	        #            ...
370	        #        def f2(x):
371	        #            return x  # <--at this return
372	        #        f2(f1)
373	        #
374	        # Note that for f1, `ty.captured_scopes[-1].local_scope` is the live scope of `kernel()`.
375	        # But when we return from `f2()`, the `callee_scope` is the scope of `f2`, so there
376	        # is nothing to freeze in this case.
377	        return retval
378	
379	    closure_val = retval.get_aggregate()
380	    assert isinstance(closure_val, ClosureValue)
381	
382	    depth = ty.captured_scopes[-1].depth
383	    frozen_locals = ty.func_hir.captures_by_depth[depth]
384	    frozen_captures = tuple(callee_scope.get(idx, builder.loc) for idx in frozen_locals)
385	    frozen_capture_types = tuple(v.get_type_allow_invalid() for v in frozen_captures)
386	
387	    new_closure_val = ClosureValue(
388	        default_values=closure_val.default_values,
389	        frozen_captures_by_depth=_replace_tuple_item(
390	            closure_val.frozen_captures_by_depth, depth, frozen_captures)
391	    )
392	    new_ty = ClosureTy(
393	        func_hir=ty.func_hir,
394	        default_value_types=ty.default_value_types,
395	        captured_scopes=ty.captured_scopes[:-1],
396	        frozen_capture_types_by_depth=_replace_tuple_item(
397	            ty.frozen_capture_types_by_depth, depth, frozen_capture_types),
398	    )
399	    return builder.make_aggregate(new_closure_val, new_ty)
400	
401	
402	def _replace_tuple_item(tup, idx, val):
403	    return tup[:idx] + (val,) + tup[idx+1:]
404

406	def _check_recursive_call(call_loc: Loc):
407	    depth = 1
408	    while call_loc is not None:
409	        depth += 1
410	        call_loc = call_loc.call_site
411	    if depth > MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH:
412	        raise TileRecursionError(f"Maximum recursion depth ({MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH}) reached"
413	                                 f" while inlining a function call")
414

416	_ResolvedOperand = (Var | hir.Block | hir.Function
417	                    | hir.StaticEvalExpression | StringFormat | hir.ResolvedName)
418	
419	
420	def _resolve_operand(x: hir.Operand, scope: Scope) -> _ResolvedOperand:
421	    if isinstance(x, hir.Value):
422	        return scope.hir2ir_varmap[x.id]
423	    elif isinstance(x, hir.Block | hir.Function | hir.StaticEvalExpression
424	                    | StringFormat | hir.ResolvedName):
425	        return x
426	    else:
427	        return sym2var(x, constant_only=True)

429	
430	def _bind_args(sig: inspect.Signature, func_name: str, args, kwargs,
431	               closure_defaults: Sequence[Var] | None = None) -> list[Var | tuple[Var, ...]]:
432	    try:
433	        bound_args = sig.bind(*args, **kwargs)
434	    except TypeError as e:
435	        raise TileTypeError(f"{func_name}(): {e}")
436	    ret = []
437	    for name, param in sig.parameters.items():
438	        if name in bound_args.arguments:
439	            ret.append(bound_args.arguments[name])
440	        elif param.kind == param.VAR_POSITIONAL:
441	            ret.append(())
442	        elif param.kind == param.VAR_KEYWORD:
443	            ret.append({})
444	        else:
445	            assert param.default is not param.empty
446	            if isinstance(param.default, ClosureDefaultPlaceholder):
447	                assert closure_defaults is not None
448	                default = closure_defaults[param.default.default_value_index]
449	            else:
450	                default = sym2var(param.default, constant_only=True)
451	            ret.append(default)
452	    return ret

15	def dead_code_elimination_pass(root_block: Block) -> None:
16	    # Build a data-flow `graph` as a dictionary.
17	    # Each key is a name of a "consumer" variable, and its value is a sequence of its dependencies.
18	    # Additionally, we build a set named `used` that initially includes all variables that
19	    # are used as immediate inputs of operations with side effects.
20	    graph: Dict[str, List[str] | Tuple[str, ...]] = dict()
21	    used: Set[str] = set()
22	    op_to_cf_name: Dict[Operation, str] = dict()
23	    _build_dataflow_graph(graph, used, op_to_cf_name, root_block,
24	                          None, None, None, None)
25	
26	    # Traverse the data-flow graph to grow the set of `used` variables.
27	    _find_used_variables(graph, used)
28	
29	    # Finally, walk the IR tree to remove unused operations and variables.
30	    _prune_block(root_block, used, op_to_cf_name, loop_mask=(), end_branch_mask=())
31	
32	
33	# Each Loop and IfElse is assigned a unique pseudovariable name of the form "$cf.<NUMBER>",
34	# so that it can participate in the data-flow graph.  The mapping from Operation to
35	# the pseudovariable name is maintained in the `op_to_cf_name` dictionary.
36	#
37	# This enables us to decide whether Loop and IfElse, as well as the corresponding Break/Continue
38	# statements, need to stay.
39	#
40	# Here are the rules for creating edges to/from the "$cf" nodes (identifiers in parentheses are
41	# rule names):
42	#
43	#   (CF_COND)
44	#       IfElse depends on its `cond` variable; a for Loop depends on its `start/stop/step`.
45	#       For example, `$cf.1` will depend on `$1` here:
46	#
47	#           if $1:    [$cf.1]
48	#               ...then-block...
49	#           else:
50	#               ...else-block...
51	#
52	#   (CF_NESTED)
53	#       Nested operation depends on its parent Loop/IfElse.
54	#       In this example, `$1` and `$cf.2` will both depend on `$cf.1`:
55	#
56	#           loop:  [$cf.1]
57	#               $1 = tile_load(...)
58	#               if $1:    [$cf.2]
59	#                   ...
60	#
61	#   (CF_DEFINED_VARS)
62	#       Loop body and result variables depend on the loop;
63	#       IfElse result variables depend on the IfElse operation.
64	#
65	#       In this example, `a.2` and `a.3` depend on `$cf.1`:
66	#
67	#           a.3 = loop (with a.2 = ...):   [$cf.1]
68	#               ...body...
69	#
70	#   (CF_BREAK_CONTINUE)
71	#       Break or Continue operation makes its loop depend on the innermost control op
72	#       that contains the Break/Continue operation.
73	#
74	#       This, together with the CF_NESTED rule, ensures that if a loop stays in the program,
75	#       so do all its Break/Continue ops, no matter how deeply nested inside IfElse statements.
76	#
77	#       In the following example, `$cf.1` depends on `$cf.3` because of the `continue` statement:
78	#
79	#           loop:  [$cf.1]
80	#              if foo:   [$cf.2]
81	#                  if bar:   [$cf.3]
82	#                      continue
83	#
84	def _make_control_flow_name(ctx: IRContext) -> str:
85	    return ctx.make_var("$cf", Loc.unknown()).name
86	
87	
88	def _build_dataflow_graph(graph: Dict[str, List[str] | Tuple[str, ...]],
89	                          used: Set[str],
90	                          op_to_cf_name: Dict[Operation, str],
91	                          block: Block,
92	                          innermost_loop: Optional[Loop],
93	                          innermost_loop_name: Optional[str],
94	                          innermost_end_branch_target: Optional[IfElse | TileReduce | TileScan],
95	                          innermost_cf_name: Optional[str]):
96	    for op in block:
97	        if isinstance(op, Loop):
98	            cf_name = _make_control_flow_name(block.ctx)
99	            op_to_cf_name[op] = cf_name
100	            graph[cf_name] = []
101	
102	            # See rule `CF_DEFINED_VARS`
103	            for init_var, body_var, res_var in zip(op.initial_values, op.body_vars, op.result_vars,
104	                                                   strict=True):
105	                graph[body_var.name] = [init_var.name, cf_name]
106	                graph[res_var.name] = [cf_name]
107	
108	            # See rule `CF_NESTED`
109	            if innermost_cf_name is not None:
110	                graph[cf_name].append(innermost_cf_name)
111	
112	            if op.is_for_loop:
113	                # See rule `CF_COND`
114	                graph[cf_name].append(op.start.name)
115	                graph[cf_name].append(op.stop.name)
116	                graph[cf_name].append(op.step.name)
117	
118	                # `For` loop can run for zero iterations, which means that initial values
119	                # of loop variables may flow directly into the loop's result variables.
120	                for res_var, init_var in zip(op.result_vars, op.initial_values, strict=True):
121	                    graph[res_var.name].append(init_var.name)
122	
123	            _build_dataflow_graph(graph, used, op_to_cf_name, op.body,
124	                                  op, cf_name, None, cf_name)
125	        elif isinstance(op, Continue):
126	            assert innermost_loop_name is not None
127	
128	            # See rule `CF_BREAK_CONTINUE`.
129	            graph[innermost_loop_name].append(innermost_cf_name)
130	
131	            # "Next" values feed into the body variables of the next iteration
132	            for body_var, next_var in zip(innermost_loop.body_vars, op.values, strict=True):
133	                graph[body_var.name].append(next_var.name)
134	
135	            # In a `for` loop, "next" values can also feed into the loop's results.
136	            # That's because the loop can immediately exit if the iterator has been exhausted.
137	            if innermost_loop.is_for_loop:
138	                for res_var, next_var in zip(innermost_loop.result_vars, op.values, strict=True):
139	                    graph[res_var.name].append(next_var.name)
140	        elif isinstance(op, Break):
141	            assert innermost_loop_name is not None
142	
143	            # See rule `CF_BREAK_CONTINUE`.
144	            graph[innermost_loop_name].append(innermost_cf_name)
145	
146	            # "Output" values feed into the loop's result variables
147	            for res_var, out_var in zip(innermost_loop.result_vars, op.values, strict=True):
148	                graph[res_var.name].append(out_var.name)
149	        elif isinstance(op, IfElse):
150	            cf_name = _make_control_flow_name(block.ctx)
151	            op_to_cf_name[op] = cf_name
152	
153	            # See rule `CF_COND`
154	            graph[cf_name] = [op.cond.name]
155	
156	            # See rule `CF_NESTED`
157	            if innermost_cf_name is not None:
158	                graph[cf_name].append(innermost_cf_name)
159	
160	            # See rule `CF_DEFINED_VARS`
161	            for res_var in op.result_vars:
162	                graph[res_var.name] = [cf_name]
163	
164	            _build_dataflow_graph(graph, used, op_to_cf_name, op.then_block,
165	                                  innermost_loop, innermost_loop_name, op, cf_name)
166	            _build_dataflow_graph(graph, used, op_to_cf_name, op.else_block,
167	                                  innermost_loop, innermost_loop_name, op, cf_name)
168	        elif isinstance(op, TileReduce | TileScan):
169	            cf_name = _make_control_flow_name(block.ctx)
170	            op_to_cf_name[op] = cf_name
171	
172	            graph[cf_name] = []
173	
174	            # See rule `CF_NESTED`
175	            if innermost_cf_name is not None:
176	                graph[cf_name].append(innermost_cf_name)
177	
178	            for x, res_var, a, b in zip(op.xs, op.result_vars, op.lhs, op.rhs, strict=True):
179	                graph[a.name] = [x.name, res_var.name]
180	                graph[b.name] = [x.name, res_var.name]
181	                # See rule `CF_DEFINED_VARS`
182	                graph[res_var.name] = [cf_name, x.name, a.name, b.name]
183	
184	            _build_dataflow_graph(graph, used, op_to_cf_name, op.body,
185	                                  None, None, op, cf_name)
186	
187	        elif isinstance(op, EndBranch):
188	            # Yielded values flow into the IfElse/TileReduce's result variables.
189	            for res_var, out_var in zip(innermost_end_branch_target.result_vars, op.outputs,
190	                                        strict=True):
191	                graph[res_var.name].append(out_var.name)
192	        else:
193	            deps = tuple(v.name for v in op.all_inputs())
194	
195	            # See rule `CF_NESTED`.
196	            if innermost_cf_name is not None:
197	                deps += (innermost_cf_name,)
198	
199	            if _must_keep(op):
200	                used.update(deps)
201	
202	            for dst_var in op.result_vars:
203	                graph[dst_var.name] = deps
204	
205	
206	def _must_keep(op: Operation) -> bool:
207	    return op.memory_effect == MemoryEffect.STORE or isinstance(op, Return)
208	
209	
210	def _find_used_variables(dataflow_graph: Dict[str, Sequence[str]], used: Set[str]):
211	    pending = list(used)
212	    while pending:
213	        dst = pending.pop()
214	        for src in dataflow_graph.get(dst, ()):
215	            if src not in used:
216	                used.add(src)
217	                pending.append(src)
218	
219	
220	def _prune_block(block: Block,
221	                 used_vars: Set[str],
222	                 op_to_cf_name: Dict[Operation, str],
223	                 loop_mask: Tuple[bool, ...],
224	                 end_branch_mask: Tuple[bool, ...]):
225	    new_ops = []
226	    for op in block.operations:
227	        if isinstance(op, Loop):
228	            if op_to_cf_name[op] in used_vars:
229	                mask = tuple(body_var.name in used_vars or res_var.name in used_vars
230	                             for body_var, res_var in zip(op.body_vars, op.result_vars,
231	                                                          strict=True))
232	                new_initial_values = _select_by_mask(op.initial_values, mask)
233	                new_initial_values = _replace_pruned_with_dummy(
234	                        new_initial_values, used_vars, new_ops)
235	                new_result_vars = _select_by_mask(op.result_vars, mask)
236	                _prune_block(op.body, used_vars, op_to_cf_name, mask, ())
237	                new_body_vars = _select_by_mask(op.body_vars, mask)
238	                op.body.params = ((op.body.params[0], *new_body_vars)
239	                                  if op.is_for_loop else new_body_vars)
240	                new_ops.append(dataclasses.replace(op,
241	                                                   initial_values=new_initial_values,
242	                                                   result_vars=new_result_vars))
243	        elif isinstance(op, Continue):
244	            next_vars = _select_by_mask(op.values, loop_mask)
245	            next_vars = _replace_pruned_with_dummy(next_vars, used_vars, new_ops)
246	            new_ops.append(Continue(result_vars=(), loc=op.loc, values=next_vars))
247	        elif isinstance(op, Break):
248	            output_vars = _select_by_mask(op.values, loop_mask)
249	            output_vars = _replace_pruned_with_dummy(output_vars, used_vars, new_ops)
250	            new_ops.append(Break(result_vars=(), loc=op.loc, values=output_vars))
251	        elif isinstance(op, IfElse):
252	            if op_to_cf_name[op] in used_vars:
253	                mask = tuple(v.name in used_vars for v in op.result_vars)
254	                _prune_block(op.then_block, used_vars, op_to_cf_name, loop_mask, mask)
255	                _prune_block(op.else_block, used_vars, op_to_cf_name, loop_mask, mask)
256	                new_result_vars = _select_by_mask(op.result_vars, mask)
257	                new_ops.append(dataclasses.replace(op, result_vars=new_result_vars))
258	        elif isinstance(op, TileReduce):
259	            if op_to_cf_name[op] in used_vars:
260	                mask = tuple(v.name in used_vars for v in op.result_vars)
261	                _prune_block(op.body, used_vars, op_to_cf_name, (), mask)
262	                new_xs = _select_by_mask(op.xs, mask)
263	                new_identities = _select_by_mask(op.identities, mask)
264	                new_lhs = _select_by_mask(op.lhs, mask)
265	                new_rhs = _select_by_mask(op.rhs, mask)
266	                op.body.params = new_lhs + new_rhs
267	                new_result_vars = _select_by_mask(op.result_vars, mask)
268	                new_ops.append(TileReduce(xs=new_xs, identities=new_identities, axis=op.axis,
269	                                          body=op.body, result_vars=new_result_vars, loc=op.loc))
270	        elif isinstance(op, TileScan):
271	            if op_to_cf_name[op] in used_vars:
272	                mask = tuple(v.name in used_vars for v in op.result_vars)
273	                _prune_block(op.body, used_vars, op_to_cf_name, (), mask)
274	                new_xs = _select_by_mask(op.xs, mask)
275	                new_identities = _select_by_mask(op.identities, mask)
276	                new_lhs = _select_by_mask(op.lhs, mask)
277	                new_rhs = _select_by_mask(op.rhs, mask)
278	                op.body.params = new_lhs + new_rhs
279	                new_result_vars = _select_by_mask(op.result_vars, mask)
280	                new_ops.append(TileScan(xs=new_xs, identities=new_identities, axis=op.axis,
281	                                        reverse=op.reverse, body=op.body,
282	                                        result_vars=new_result_vars, loc=op.loc))
283	        elif isinstance(op, EndBranch):
284	            output_vars = _select_by_mask(op.outputs, end_branch_mask)
285	            new_ops.append(dataclasses.replace(op, outputs=output_vars))
286	        elif any(r.name in used_vars for r in op.result_vars) or _must_keep(op):
287	            new_ops.append(op)
288	    block.operations = new_ops

291	# We keep a carried variable as long as its body variable or its result variable is used.
292	#
293	# This may leave some initial/continuation/break values referencing vars whose defining
294	# ops have been pruned. This function replaces those with MakeDummy() ops
295	# so that bytecode generation can proceed normally.
296	#
297	# Example 1:
298	# ==========
299	#   In the following case, the result variable `x.2` is used,
300	#   therefore `x` must be kept as a carried variable:
301	#
302	#       Source code                       IR
303	#       ===========                       ===============
304	#       x = ct.ones(...)                  x = tile_ones(...)
305	#       while True:                       x.2 = loop (with x.1 = x):
306	#           x = ct.load(...)                  x.3 = tile_load(...)
307	#           ct.store(..., x)                  tile_store(..., x.3)
308	#           if ...:                           if ...:
309	#               break                             break x.3
310	#           x = x + 1                         x.4 = x.3 + 1
311	#                                             continue x.4
312	#       ct.store(..., x)                  tile_store(..., x.2)
313	#
314	#   However, the body variable `x.1` is unused. This also makes the initial variable `x`,
315	#   as well as the continuation value `x.4`, unused. Thus, dead code elimination will prune
316	#   the program as such:
317	#
318	#       x.2 = loop (with x.1 = <pruned>):
319	#           x.3 = tile_load(...)
320	#           tile_store(..., x.3)
321	#           if ...:
322	#               break x.3
323	#           continue <pruned>
324	#       tile_store(..., x.2)
325	#
326	#
327	# Example 2:
328	# ==========
329	#   Consider the opposite case, where the body variable is used but the result variable
330	#   isn't:
331	#
332	#       Source code                       IR
333	#       ===========                       ===============
334	#       x = ct.ones(...)                  x = tile_ones(...)
335	#       while True:                       x.2 = loop (with x.1 = x):
336	#           ct.store(..., x)                  tile_store(..., x.1)
337	#           x = x + 1                         x.3 = x.1 + 1
338	#           if ...:                           x.4 = if ...:
339	#               x = x + 2                         x.5 = x.3 + 2
340	#               break                             break x.5
341	#                                             else:
342	#                                                 yield x.3
343	#                                             continue x.4
344	#       # x is never used afterward
345	#
346	#   Since `x.2` is unused, `x.5` is also unused. Thus, the pruned IR looks like so:
347	#
348	#       x = tile_ones(...)
349	#       x.2 = loop (with x.1 = x):
350	#           tile_store(..., x.1)
351	#           x.3 = x.1 + 1
352	#           x.4 = if ...:
353	#               break <pruned>
354	#           else:
355	#               yield x.3
356	#         continue x.4
357	#
358	def _replace_pruned_with_dummy(vars: Sequence[Var],
359	                               used_vars: Set[str],
360	                               new_ops: list[Operation]) -> tuple[Var, ...]:
361	    return tuple(v if v.name in used_vars else _make_dummy_like(v, new_ops)
362	                 for v in vars)
363	
364	
365	def _make_dummy_like(v: Var, new_ops: list[Operation]) -> Var:
366	    dummy_var = v.ctx.make_var_like(v)
367	    dummy_var.set_type(v.get_type())
368	    new_ops.append(MakeDummy(result_vars=(dummy_var,), loc=dummy_var.loc))
369	    return dummy_var
370	
371	
372	def _select_by_mask(seq: Sequence[Any], mask: Sequence[bool]) -> Tuple[Any, ...]:
373	    return tuple(x for x, keep in zip(seq, mask, strict=True) if keep)

14	def hoist_loop_invariants(root_block: Block):
15	    def_depth = {p.name: 0 for p in root_block.params}
16	    _hoist(root_block, [], def_depth, False)
17

19	# Indicates whether a block could in theory be moved, based purely on the classes of operations
20	# contained within (i.e. whether it contains any side effects or jumps).
21	# It does not take actual data dependencies into account.
22	class _BlockMobility(enum.IntEnum):
23	    # Under no circumstances the block (or any of its ancestors) could be moved.
24	    # For example, this happens when the block contains an operation with a side effect.
25	    IMMOVABLE = 0
26	
27	    # The block itself can't be hoisted by itself, but the loop that contains still may.
28	    # This happens when the block contains a Continue or a Break statement.
29	    CAN_MOVE_WITH_LOOP = 1
30	
31	    # The block can move. Note that it may still not be possible to hoist it,
32	    # depending on whether it depends on values defined inside its parent loop.
33	    CAN_MOVE = 2
34	
35	
36	@dataclass
37	class _BlockResult:
38	    mobility: _BlockMobility = _BlockMobility.CAN_MOVE
39	
40	    # Minimum depth to which the block could be hoisted, based on its data dependencies
41	    min_depth: int = 0
42	
43	
44	# Helper class for accumulating data dependency information per operation.
45	@dataclass
46	class _DependencyInfo:
47	    # True if this operation can't be hoisted
48	    must_stay: bool
49	
50	    # Maximum depth of this operation's data dependencies' definitions, excluding
51	    # the block where the operation is originally defined.
52	    # I.e., this is the minimum depth to which we could hoist the operation,
53	    # based solely on its data dependencies.
54	    max_outside_depth: int = 0
55	
56	    def update(self, dependency_depth: int, cur_block_depth: int):
57	        if dependency_depth >= cur_block_depth:
58	            # If the operation depends on another operation in its block, it can't be hoisted.
59	            # But also it doesn't affect max_outside_depth in case the whole block is moved at once.
60	            self.must_stay = True
61	        else:
62	            # Otherwise, we update the maximum data dependency depth.
63	            self.max_outside_depth = max(self.max_outside_depth, dependency_depth)
64	
65	
66	@dataclass
67	class _StackItem:
68	    new_block: Block
69	    is_loop_body: bool
70	
71	
72	# This function does too many things at once. However, this allows us to do everything
73	# in a single linear-time pass, no matter how many nested loops we may have.
74	def _hoist(block: Block, stack: list[_StackItem], def_depth: dict[str, int], is_loop_body: bool) \
75	        -> _BlockResult:
76	    depth = len(stack)
77	    new_block = block.empty_like_self()
78	    stack.append(_StackItem(new_block, is_loop_body))
79	    ret = _BlockResult()
80	
81	    for op in block:
82	        # We can only hoist operations out of loops, not other nested blocks like IfElse branches.
83	        depinfo = _DependencyInfo(must_stay=not is_loop_body)
84	
85	        if isinstance(op, Loop | TileReduce | TileScan):
86	            for var in op.body.params:
87	                def_depth[var.name] = depth + 1
88	
89	            body_res = _hoist(op.body, stack, def_depth, True)
90	            if body_res.mobility == _BlockMobility.IMMOVABLE:
91	                # Propagate IMMOVABLE to all ancestors.
92	                ret.mobility = _BlockMobility.IMMOVABLE
93	                depinfo.must_stay = True
94	
95	            inputs = op.initial_values if isinstance(op, Loop) else op.xs
96	            for v in inputs:
97	                depinfo.update(def_depth[v.name], depth)
98	            depinfo.update(body_res.min_depth, depth)
99	        elif isinstance(op, IfElse):
100	            depinfo.update(def_depth[op.cond.name], depth)
101	            for branch in (op.then_block, op.else_block):
102	                branch_res = _hoist(branch, stack, def_depth, False)
103	                depinfo.update(branch_res.min_depth, depth)
104	                if branch_res.mobility != _BlockMobility.CAN_MOVE:
105	                    # Propagate CAN_MOVE_WITH_LOOP and IMMOVABLE
106	                    ret.mobility = min(ret.mobility, branch_res.mobility)
107	                    depinfo.must_stay = True
108	        elif op.memory_effect == MemoryEffect.STORE or isinstance(op, Return):
109	            ret.mobility = _BlockMobility.IMMOVABLE
110	            depinfo.must_stay = True
111	        elif isinstance(op, (Continue, Break)):
112	            # Can't move the block that contains a Continue/Break, unless it is moved
113	            # together with its containing loop.
114	            ret.mobility = min(ret.mobility, _BlockMobility.CAN_MOVE_WITH_LOOP)
115	            depinfo.must_stay = True
116	        elif isinstance(op, EndBranch):
117	            depinfo.must_stay = True
118	            for v in op.outputs:
119	                depinfo.update(def_depth[v.name], depth)
120	        else:
121	            # "Pure" operation without any nested blocks, side effects and jumps.
122	            assert len(op.nested_blocks) == 0
123	            for v in op.all_inputs():
124	                depinfo.update(def_depth[v.name], depth)
125	
126	        target_depth = depth
127	        if depinfo.must_stay:
128	            ret.min_depth = max(ret.min_depth, depinfo.max_outside_depth)
129	        else:
130	            while target_depth > depinfo.max_outside_depth and stack[target_depth].is_loop_body:
131	                target_depth -= 1
132	
133	        stack[target_depth].new_block.append(op)
134	
135	        # Record the definition depth of the results variables. Note that we do this
136	        # after hoisting, so that for any subsequent operations, we compute max_outside_depth
137	        # based on the target depth, not on the original depth.
138	        for v in op.result_vars:
139	            def_depth[v.name] = target_depth
140	
141	    stack.pop()
142	    block[:] = new_block.detach_all()
143	    return ret

32	@dataclass(frozen=True)
33	class DataPredicate:
34	    alias_set: AliasSet
35	    div_by: int
36	    may_alias_internally: bool
37	
38	    def unify(self, other: "DataPredicate") -> "DataPredicate":
39	        return DataPredicate(
40	                alias_set=self.alias_set | other.alias_set,
41	                div_by=gcd(self.div_by, other.div_by),
42	                may_alias_internally=self.may_alias_internally | other.may_alias_internally
43	                )
44	
45	    def replace(self, **key_value_pairs) -> "DataPredicate":
46	        return dataclasses.replace(self, **key_value_pairs)

49	@dataclass
50	class DataflowResult:
51	    predicates: Dict[str, DataPredicate]

53	    def __getitem__(self, var_name: str) -> DataPredicate:
54	        return self.predicates[var_name]

57	def dataflow_analysis(root_block: Block,
58	                      parameter_constraints: Sequence[tuple[tuple[Var, ...], ParameterConstraint]]
59	                      ) -> DataflowResult:
60	    state = _State(_Tracker(), _Tracker())
61	    alias_set_mapper = _AliasSetMapper()
62	    for flat_params, constraint in parameter_constraints:
63	        if isinstance(constraint, ArrayConstraint):
64	            predicates = _get_array_predicates(constraint, alias_set_mapper)
65	            for var, pred in zip(flat_params, predicates, strict=True):
66	                state.tracker.update(var, pred)
67	                state.list_array_tracker.update(var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
68	        elif isinstance(constraint, ListConstraint):
69	            assert isinstance(constraint.element, ArrayConstraint)
70	            assert len(flat_params) == 2
71	            base_ptr, size_var = flat_params
72	            state.tracker.update(base_ptr,
73	                                 DataPredicate(alias_set=alias_set_mapper(constraint.alias_groups),
74	                                               div_by=1,
75	                                               may_alias_internally=constraint.elements_may_alias))
76	            elt_predicates = _get_array_predicates(constraint.element, alias_set_mapper)
77	            agg_pred = _AggregatePredicate(dict(enumerate(elt_predicates)))
78	            state.list_array_tracker.update(base_ptr, agg_pred)
79	            state.set_always_true(size_var)
80	        elif isinstance(constraint, ScalarConstraint):
81	            [only_param] = flat_params
82	            state.set_always_true(only_param)
83	        else:
84	            assert False
85	
86	    _analyze_aliases_in_block(root_block, state, None, None)
87	
88	    while state.dirty:
89	        state.reset_dirty()
90	        _analyze_aliases_in_block(root_block, state, None, None)
91	
92	    return DataflowResult(state.tracker.finalize())

95	def _get_array_predicates(constraint: ArrayConstraint, alias_set_mapper: "_AliasSetMapper"):
96	    # Base pointer predicate
97	    ret = [DataPredicate(alias_set=alias_set_mapper(constraint.alias_groups),
98	                         div_by=constraint.base_addr_divisible_by,
99	                         may_alias_internally=constraint.may_alias_internally)]
100	
101	    # Shape predicates
102	    for div_by in constraint.shape_divisible_by:
103	        ret.append(DataPredicate(alias_set=ALIAS_UNIVERSE, div_by=div_by,
104	                                 may_alias_internally=True))
105	
106	    # Stride predicates
107	    for static, div_by in zip(constraint.stride_constant,
108	                              constraint.stride_divisible_by, strict=True):
109	        if static is not None:
110	            div_by = static
111	        ret.append(DataPredicate(alias_set=ALIAS_UNIVERSE, div_by=div_by,
112	                                 may_alias_internally=True))
113	    return ret

115	
116	class _AliasSetMapper:
117	    def __init__(self):
118	        self._bit_seq = map(lambda x: 1 << x, itertools.count())
119	        self._mapping: dict[str, int] = dict()
120	
121	    def __call__(self, alias_groups: Sequence[str] | None) -> AliasSet:
122	        if alias_groups is None:
123	            return ALIAS_UNIVERSE
124	        elif len(alias_groups) == 0:
125	            return next(self._bit_seq)
126	        else:
127	            ret = 0
128	            for ap in alias_groups:
129	                bit = self._mapping.get(ap)
130	                if bit is None:
131	                    bit = next(self._bit_seq)
132	                    self._mapping[ap] = bit
133	                ret |= bit
134	            return ret
135	
136	
137	ALWAYS_TRUE_PREDICATE = DataPredicate(alias_set=ALIAS_UNIVERSE,
138	                                      div_by=1,
139	                                      may_alias_internally=True)

142	class _AggregatePredicate:
143	    def __init__(self, items: Dict[Any, DataPredicate]):
144	        self._items = items
145	
146	    def __getitem__(self, key):
147	        return self._items.get(key, ALWAYS_TRUE_PREDICATE)
148	
149	    def unify(self, other: "_AggregatePredicate") -> "_AggregatePredicate":
150	        all_keys = set(self._items.keys()) | other._items.keys()
151	        unified_items = {k: self[k].unify(other[k]) for k in all_keys}
152	        return _AggregatePredicate(unified_items)
153	
154	    def __eq__(self, other: "_AggregatePredicate") -> bool:
155	        all_keys = set(self._items.keys()) | other._items.keys()
156	        return all(self[k] == other[k] for k in all_keys)
157	
158	
159	ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE = _AggregatePredicate({})

162	P = TypeVar("P")

164	
165	class _Tracker(Generic[P]):
166	    def __init__(self):
167	        self.dirty = False
168	        self._predicates: dict[str, P] = dict()
169	
170	    def __getitem__(self, var: Var) -> P:
171	        return self._predicates[var.name]
172	
173	    def update(self, var: Var, pred: P):
174	        old_pred = self._predicates.get(var.name)
175	        if old_pred is None:
176	            new_pred = pred
177	        else:
178	            new_pred = old_pred.unify(pred)
179	            if new_pred == old_pred:
180	                return
181	        self.dirty = True
182	        self._predicates[var.name] = new_pred
183	
184	    def propagate(self, src: Var, dst: Var):
185	        self.update(dst, self[src])
186	
187	    def finalize(self) -> dict[str, P]:
188	        return self._predicates

191	@dataclass
192	class _State:
193	    tracker: _Tracker[DataPredicate]
194	    list_array_tracker: _Tracker[_AggregatePredicate]
195	
196	    def propagate(self, src: Var, dst: Var):
197	        self.tracker.propagate(src, dst)
198	        self.list_array_tracker.propagate(src, dst)
199	
200	    def set_always_true(self, var: Var):
201	        self.tracker.update(var, ALWAYS_TRUE_PREDICATE)
202	        self.list_array_tracker.update(var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
203	
204	    @property
205	    def dirty(self):
206	        return self.tracker.dirty or self.list_array_tracker.dirty
207	
208	    def reset_dirty(self):
209	        self.tracker.dirty = False
210	        self.list_array_tracker.dirty = False

213	def _get_divisibility_for_binary_op(op: RawBinaryArithmeticOperation,
214	                                    tracker: _Tracker) -> int:
215	    result_dtype = get_dtype(op.result_var.get_type())
216	    if is_integral(result_dtype):
217	        x_div = tracker[op.lhs].div_by
218	        y_div = tracker[op.rhs].div_by
219	        if op.fn in ('add', 'sub'):
220	            return gcd(x_div, y_div)
221	        elif op.fn == 'mul':
222	            return x_div * y_div
223	    return 1
224	
225	
226	def _get_divisibility_for_unary_op(op: Unary, tracker: _Tracker) -> int:
227	    result_dtype = get_dtype(op.result_var.get_type())
228	    if is_integral(result_dtype):
229	        x_div = tracker[op.operand].div_by
230	        if op.fn in ('abs', 'neg'):
231	            return x_div
232	    return 1
233	
234	
235	def _analyze_aliases_in_block(block: Block,
236	                              state: _State,
237	                              innermost_loop: Loop | None,
238	                              innermost_branch: IfElse | TileReduce | TileScan | None):
239	    for op in block.operations:
240	        if isinstance(op, Assign):
241	            state.propagate(op.value, op.result_var)
242	        elif isinstance(op, AssumeDivBy):
243	            new_pred = state.tracker[op.x].replace(div_by=op.divisor)
244	            state.tracker.update(op.result_var, new_pred)
245	            state.list_array_tracker.propagate(op.x, op.result_var)
246	        elif isinstance(op, GetArrayListItem):
247	            # TODO: more granular array list get item alias analysis
248	            # Propagate to the base pointer of the array
249	            list_val = op.x.get_aggregate()
250	            assert isinstance(list_val, ListValue)
251	            arr_predicates = state.list_array_tracker[list_val.base_ptr]
252	            for i, var in enumerate(op.result_vars):
253	                state.tracker.update(var, arr_predicates[i])
254	                state.list_array_tracker.update(var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
255	        elif isinstance(op, PointerOffset):
256	            # Update divby on pointer We only update divby on a pointer of 0d,
257	            # (Array.slice) Divby of a block of pointers (like ptr + arange for
258	            # ct.gather) are handled by tileiras.
259	            ptr_ty = op.pointer.get_type()
260	            assert isinstance(ptr_ty, TileTy)
261	            ptr_info = PointerInfo(ptr_ty.dtype)
262	            if all(x == 1 for x in ptr_ty.shape):
263	                bitwidth = ptr_info.pointee_dtype.bitwidth
264	                BYTE_BITWIDTH = 8
265	                if bitwidth % BYTE_BITWIDTH == 0:
266	                    offset_divby = state.tracker[op.offset].div_by * (bitwidth // BYTE_BITWIDTH)
267	                else:
268	                    offset_divby = 1
269	                ptr_divby = state.tracker[op.pointer].div_by
270	                new_divby = gcd(ptr_divby, offset_divby)
271	            else:
272	                new_divby = 1
273	            pred = state.tracker[op.pointer].replace(div_by=new_divby)
274	            state.tracker.update(op.result_var, pred)
275	            state.list_array_tracker.update(op.result_var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
276	        elif isinstance(op, TileBroadcast | TileReshape | AssumeBounded):
277	            # Needed for tiles of pointers produced by gather/scatter
278	            state.propagate(op.x, op.result_var)
279	        elif isinstance(op, TileAsType):
280	            orig_dtype = get_dtype(op.x.get_type())
281	            result_dtype = get_dtype(op.result_var.get_type())
282	            div_by = state.tracker[op.x].div_by
283	            if is_integral(orig_dtype) and is_integral(result_dtype):
284	                # Truncate to int dtype is the same as mod 2^bitwidth
285	                div_by = gcd(div_by, 1 << result_dtype.bitwidth)
286	            else:
287	                div_by = 1
288	            pred = state.tracker[op.x].replace(div_by=div_by)
289	            state.tracker.update(op.result_var, pred)
290	            state.list_array_tracker.update(op.result_var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
291	        elif isinstance(op, TypedConst):
292	            if isinstance(op.value, int):
293	                div_by = abs(op.value)
294	                state.tracker.update(op.result_var, DataPredicate(alias_set=ALIAS_UNIVERSE,
295	                                                                  div_by=div_by,
296	                                                                  may_alias_internally=True))
297	                state.list_array_tracker.update(op.result_var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
298	            else:
299	                state.set_always_true(op.result_var)
300	        elif isinstance(op, RawBinaryArithmeticOperation):
301	            new_div_by = _get_divisibility_for_binary_op(op, state.tracker)
302	            pred = ALWAYS_TRUE_PREDICATE.replace(div_by=new_div_by)
303	            state.tracker.update(op.result_var, pred)
304	            state.list_array_tracker.update(op.result_var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
305	        elif isinstance(op, Unary):
306	            new_div_by = _get_divisibility_for_unary_op(op, state.tracker)
307	            pred = ALWAYS_TRUE_PREDICATE.replace(div_by=new_div_by)
308	            state.tracker.update(op.result_var, pred)
309	            state.list_array_tracker.update(op.result_var, ALWAYS_TRUE_AGG_PREDICATE)
310	        elif isinstance(op, Loop):
311	            if op.is_for_loop:
312	                state.set_always_true(op.induction_var)
313	
314	            for init, body, result in zip(op.initial_values, op.body_vars, op.result_vars,
315	                                          strict=True):
316	                # Loop initial values flow into body values.
317	                state.propagate(init, body)
318	
319	                # `For` loop initial values can flow into result values if
320	                # loop runs for 0 iteration.
321	                if op.is_for_loop:
322	                    state.propagate(init, result)
323	
324	            _analyze_aliases_in_block(op.body, state, op, None)
325	        elif isinstance(op, Continue):
326	            for next, body, result in zip(op.values, innermost_loop.body_vars,
327	                                          innermost_loop.result_vars, strict=True):
328	                # Loop next values can flow into body values
329	                state.propagate(next, body)
330	
331	                # `For` loop next values can flow into result values when
332	                # the iterator is exhausted.
333	                if innermost_loop.is_for_loop:
334	                    state.propagate(next, result)
335	
336	        elif isinstance(op, Break):
337	            for output, result in zip(op.values, innermost_loop.result_vars, strict=True):
338	                state.propagate(output, result)
339	
340	        elif isinstance(op, IfElse):
341	            _analyze_aliases_in_block(op.then_block, state, innermost_loop, op)
342	
343	            _analyze_aliases_in_block(op.else_block, state, innermost_loop, op)
344	
345	        elif isinstance(op, EndBranch):
346	            for output, result in zip(op.outputs, innermost_branch.result_vars, strict=True):
347	                state.propagate(output, result)
348	
349	        elif isinstance(op, TileReduce | TileScan):
350	            for v in op.body.params:
351	                state.set_always_true(v)
352	            _analyze_aliases_in_block(op.body, state, None, op)
353	
354	        else:
355	            assert len(op.nested_blocks) == 0
356	            for v in op.result_vars:
357	                state.set_always_true(v)

6	
7	from cuda.tile._ir.ir import Block, Operation, Var
8	from cuda.tile._ir.ops import AssumeDivBy, MakeTensorView, LoadPointer, StorePointer
9	from cuda.tile._passes.dataflow_analysis import DataflowResult, DataPredicate
10	
11	_OPS_NEED_ASSUME = (MakeTensorView, LoadPointer, StorePointer)
12	
13	
14	def add_divby_pass(root_block: Block, df_result: DataflowResult):
15	    candidates = set()
16	    _scan_block(root_block, candidates)
17	    var_map = {}
18	    _rewrite_block(root_block, df_result, var_map, candidates)
19	
20	
21	def _scan_block(block: Block, candidates: set[str]):
22	    """Add var that needs divby to candidates"""
23	    for op in block:
24	        if isinstance(op, _OPS_NEED_ASSUME):
25	            for var in op.all_inputs():
26	                candidates.add(var.name)
27	        for b in op.nested_blocks:
28	            _scan_block(b, candidates)
29	
30	
31	def _rewrite_block(block: Block,
32	                   df_result: DataflowResult,
33	                   var_map: dict[str, Var],
34	                   candidates: set[str]):
35	    new_ops = []
36	    for param in block.params:
37	        _add_assume_divby(param, df_result, new_ops, var_map)
38	
39	    for op in block:
40	        to_assume = tuple(var for var in op.result_vars if var.name in candidates)
41	        new_ops.append(_remap_operands(op, var_map))
42	        for var in to_assume:
43	            _add_assume_divby(var, df_result, new_ops, var_map)
44	        for b in op.nested_blocks:
45	            _rewrite_block(b, df_result, var_map, candidates)
46	
47	    block[:] = new_ops
48	
49	
50	def _remap_operands(op: Operation, var_map: dict[str, Var]):
51	    new_fields = {}
52	    for field_name, var in op.operands.items():
53	        if isinstance(var, tuple):
54	            new_var = tuple(var_map.get(vi.name, vi) for vi in var)
55	            new_fields[field_name] = new_var
56	        elif isinstance(var, Var):
57	            new_var = var_map.get(var.name, var)
58	            new_fields[field_name] = new_var
59	    return dataclasses.replace(op, **new_fields)
60	
61	
62	def _add_assume_divby(x: Var,
63	                      df_result: DataflowResult,
64	                      op_list: list[Operation],
65	                      var_map: dict[str, Var]) -> Var:
66	    if x.name in var_map:
67	        return x
68	    MAX_DIVBY = 1024
69	    divisor = df_result[x.name].div_by
70	    power_of_2_d = min(divisor & -divisor, MAX_DIVBY)
71	    if power_of_2_d > 1:
72	        result_var = x.ctx.make_var_like(x)
73	        result_var.set_type(x.get_type())
74	        op = AssumeDivBy(divisor=power_of_2_d,
75	                         x=x,
76	                         result_vars=(result_var,), loc=x.loc)
77	        op_list.append(op)
78	        var_map[x.name] = result_var
79	        old_pred = df_result[x.name]
80	        df_result.predicates[result_var.name] = DataPredicate(
81	            alias_set=old_pred.alias_set,
82	            div_by=power_of_2_d,
83	            may_alias_internally=old_pred.may_alias_internally
84	            )
85	        return result_var
86	    return x
87

26	    # Unwrap the @function decorator
27	    while is_function_wrapper(pyfunc):
28	        pyfunc = pyfunc.__wrapped__
29	
30	    # Use findsource() instead of getsourcelines() because the latter unwraps any decorators,
31	    # which we don't want.
32	    file_source_lines, first_line = inspect.findsource(pyfunc)
33	    source_lines = inspect.getblock(file_source_lines[first_line:])
34	    first_line += 1
35	
36	    # The source code of our function could be inside a class, an if-else block etc.
37	    # This means it can have extra indentation on the left. If we try to give it
38	    # to ast.parse() as is, we will get a parse error. The common workaround
39	    # suggested on the web is to filter the source through textwrap.dedent() to remove
40	    # a common amount of indentation. This is not correct though, because lines that
41	    # only contain spaces and comments, as well as continuation lines, are not required to
42	    # be indented. For example, this code is valid:
43	    #
44	    #     class A:
45	    #         def foo(self):
46	    #              return (100 +
47	    #     200)
48	    #
49	    # The "textwrap.dedent" method would fail to remove the extra indent because the
50	    # continuation line "200)" is not indented.
51	    #
52	    # To handle this properly, we resort to a hack: add one level of indentation to our
53	    # function and wrap it inside an "if True:" block.
54	    header_line = "if True:\n "
55	    indented_source = header_line + " ".join(source_lines)
56	    mod = ast.parse(indented_source)
57	    assert len(mod.body) == 1
58	    assert isinstance(mod.body[0], ast.If)
59	    assert len(mod.body[0].body) == 1
60	    func_def = mod.body[0].body[0]
61	    assert isinstance(func_def, ast.FunctionDef)
62	    _fix_line_and_column_numbers(func_def, first_line)
63	
64	    func_globals = dict(pyfunc.__builtins__)
65	    func_globals.update(pyfunc.__globals__)
66	    # Add closure variables (from freevars)
67	    if pyfunc.__closure__:
68	        for name, cell in zip(pyfunc.__code__.co_freevars, pyfunc.__closure__):
69	            func_globals[name] = cell.cell_contents
70	
71	    filename = inspect.getfile(pyfunc)
72	    desc = FunctionDesc(func_def.name, filename, first_line, func_def.col_offset + 1,
73	                        is_entry=entry_point)
74	    local_names, _, _ = ast_get_all_local_names(func_def)
75	    ctx = _Context(filename, first_line, desc, func_globals, local_names, entry_point,
76	                   func_depth=0)
77	    signature = inspect.signature(pyfunc)
78	    ret = _get_function_hir_inner(func_def, signature, ctx)
79	
80	    _finalize_func(ret, 0, ())
81	    return ret

84	# Translate the 1-based line and 0-based column numbers of the chunk we passed to the
85	# AST parser to the original line and column numbers in the file.
86	def _fix_line_and_column_numbers(tree: ast.AST, first_line: int):
87	    for node in ast.walk(tree):
88	        if hasattr(node, "lineno"):
89	            # Why -2?
90	            #    -1 because both first_line and node.lineno are 1-based;
91	            #    another -1 to account for the "if True" line that we inserted.
92	            node.lineno += first_line - 2
93	            node.end_lineno += first_line - 2
94	
95	            # Subtract 1 from the column offset to correct for an extra level
96	            # of indentation we inserted for the dummy "if True" block.
97	            node.col_offset -= 1
98	            node.end_col_offset -= 1

101	def _collect_local_loads(block: hir.Block, result: set[ResolvedName]) -> None:
102	    # Collect the ResolvedName of every load_var that targets a local variable.
103	    for call in block.calls:
104	        if call.callee is hir_stubs.load_var:
105	            rn = call.args[0]
106	            if isinstance(rn, ResolvedName) and rn.depth >= 0:
107	                result.add(rn)
108	        for arg in call.args:
109	            if isinstance(arg, hir.Block):
110	                _collect_local_loads(arg, result)

113	def _finalize_func(func: hir.Function, depth: int,
114	                   enclosing_functions: tuple[hir.Function, ...]) -> set[ResolvedName]:
115	    func.enclosing_funcs = enclosing_functions
116	    new_enclosing = enclosing_functions + (func,)
117	    accessed: set[ResolvedName] = set()
118	    _collect_local_loads(func.body, accessed)
119	    for nested_func in func.nested_functions:
120	        accessed |= _finalize_func(nested_func, depth + 1, new_enclosing)
121	    func.captures_by_depth = tuple(
122	        tuple(sorted({rn.index for rn in accessed if rn.depth == d}))
123	        for d in range(depth)
124	    )
125	    return {rn for rn in accessed if rn.depth < depth}

128	def _get_function_hir_inner(func_def: ast.FunctionDef | ast.Lambda, signature: inspect.Signature,
129	                            ctx: "_Context") -> hir.Function:
130	    assert isinstance(func_def, ast.FunctionDef | ast.Lambda)
131	    body = _ast2hir(func_def, ctx)
132	    all_ast_args = _get_all_parameters(func_def, ctx)
133	    param_names = tuple(p.arg for p in all_ast_args)
134	    local_names = tuple(ctx._own_local_names)
135	    frozen_global_names = tuple(sorted(ctx.frozen_globals.keys()))
136	    frozen_global_values = tuple(ctx.frozen_globals[name] for name in frozen_global_names)
137	    return hir.Function(
138	        desc=ctx.function_desc,
139	        body=body,
140	        signature=signature,
141	        local_names=local_names,
142	        param_local_indices=tuple(ctx.name_to_local_idx[name] for name in param_names),
143	        param_locs=tuple(ctx.get_loc(p) for p in all_ast_args),
144	        frozen_global_names=frozen_global_names,
145	        frozen_global_values=frozen_global_values,
146	        value_id_upper_bound=next(ctx.value_id_sequence),
147	        nested_functions=tuple(ctx.nested_functions),
148	        captures_by_depth=(),  # to be filled later
149	        enclosing_funcs=(),  # to be filled later
150	    )

159	class _Context:
160	    def __init__(self, filename: str, first_line: int, function_desc: FunctionDesc,
161	                 frozen_globals: Mapping[str, Any], local_names: set[str], entry_point: bool,
162	                 func_depth: int = 0,
163	                 outer_rns: Dict[str, ResolvedName] | None = None,
164	                 own_locals: set[str] | None = None):
165	        self.filename = filename
166	        self.first_line = first_line
167	        self.function_desc = function_desc
168	        self.frozen_globals = frozen_globals
169	        self.local_names = local_names
170	        self.entry_point = entry_point
171	        self.parent_loops: List[LoopKind] = []
172	        self.current_loc = Loc.unknown()
173	        self.current_block: Optional[hir.Block] = None
174	        self.value_id_sequence = itertools.count()
175	        self.block_id_sequence = itertools.count()
176	        self.comp_acc_id_sequence = itertools.count()
177	        self.nested_functions = []
178	        # This function's depth in the nesting hierarchy.
179	        self._func_depth: int = func_depth
180	        # Flat list of own local names, position = local index; grows as comp vars are
181	        # registered. Excludes outer captures.
182	        sorted_own = sorted(own_locals if own_locals is not None else local_names)
183	        self._own_local_names: list[str] = list(sorted_own)
184	        # Map from own local name → local index.
185	        self.name_to_local_idx: dict[str, int] = {n: i for i, n in enumerate(sorted_own)}
186	        # ResolvedNames for names captured from outer scopes.
187	        self._outer_rns: dict[str, ResolvedName] = outer_rns or {}
188	        # Index map for frozen globals: name → index into frozen_global_names.
189	        self._frozen_global_idx: dict[str, int] = {
190	            n: i for i, n in enumerate(sorted(frozen_globals.keys()))
191	        }
192	        # Indices of comprehension induction variables(excluded from stored_indices).
193	        self._comp_induction_indices: set[int] = set()
194	
195	    def new_comp_induction_index(self, name: str) -> int:
196	        # Allocate a non-phi local index for a comprehension induction variable.
197	        idx = len(self._own_local_names)
198	        self._own_local_names.append(name)
199	        self._comp_induction_indices.add(idx)
200	        return idx
201	
202	    def declare_local(self, name: str) -> None:
203	        # Allocate a local index and install it in name_to_local_idx.
204	        idx = len(self._own_local_names)
205	        self._own_local_names.append(name)
206	        self.name_to_local_idx[name] = idx
207	
208	    def make_value(self) -> hir.Value:
209	        return make_value(next(self.value_id_sequence))
210	
211	    @contextmanager
212	    def change_loc(self, loc: ast.AST | Loc):
213	        old = self.current_loc
214	        self.current_loc = loc if isinstance(loc, Loc) else self.get_loc(loc)
215	        try:
216	            yield
217	        finally:
218	            self.current_loc = old
219	
220	    @contextmanager
221	    def new_block(self, params: Sequence[hir.Value] = ()):
222	        block_id = next(self.block_id_sequence)
223	        new_block = hir.Block(block_id, tuple(params), calls=[], have_result=False, result=None,
224	                              jump=None, jump_loc=Loc.unknown(),
225	                              stored_indices=set(), loc=self.current_loc)
226	        old = self.current_block
227	        self.current_block = new_block
228	        try:
229	            yield self.current_block
230	        finally:
231	            self.current_block = old
232	
233	        if old is not None:
234	            old.stored_indices.update(new_block.stored_indices)
235	
236	    def call(self, callee, args, kwargs=()) -> hir.Value:
237	        res = self.make_value()
238	        self.current_block.calls.append(hir.Call(res, callee, args, kwargs, self.current_loc))
239	        return res
240	
241	    def call_void(self, callee, args, kwargs=()) -> None:
242	        self.current_block.calls.append(hir.Call(None, callee, args, kwargs, self.current_loc))
243	
244	    def set_block_jump(self, jump: hir.Jump):
245	        assert self.current_block.jump is None
246	        self.current_block.jump = jump
247	        self.current_block.jump_loc = self.current_loc
248	
249	    def set_block_jump_with_result(self, jump: hir.Jump, result: hir.Operand):
250	        self.set_block_jump(jump)
251	        self.current_block.result = result
252	        self.current_block.have_result = True
253	
254	    def store(self, var_name: str, value: hir.Operand):
255	        index = self.name_to_local_idx.get(var_name)
256	        if index is None:
257	            raise self.syntax_error(
258	                f"Cannot assign to '{var_name}':"
259	                " assignment to a global or nonlocal variable is not supported")
260	        rn = ResolvedName(self._func_depth, index)
261	        self.call_void(hir_stubs.store_var, (rn, value))
262	        if index not in self._comp_induction_indices:
263	            self.current_block.stored_indices.add(index)
264	
265	    def load(self, var_name: str) -> hir.Value:
266	        if var_name in self.name_to_local_idx:
267	            rn = ResolvedName(self._func_depth, self.name_to_local_idx[var_name])
268	        elif var_name in self._outer_rns:
269	            rn = self._outer_rns[var_name]
270	        elif var_name in self._frozen_global_idx:
271	            rn = ResolvedName(-1, self._frozen_global_idx[var_name])
272	        else:
273	            rn = UNKNOWN_NAME
274	        return self.call(hir_stubs.load_var, (rn, var_name))
275	
276	    def get_loc(self, node: ast.AST) -> Loc:
277	        return Loc(node.lineno, node.col_offset, self.filename,
278	                   node.end_lineno, node.end_col_offset, self.function_desc)
279	
280	    def syntax_error(self, message: str, loc=None) -> TileSyntaxError:
281	        if loc is None:
282	            loc = self.current_loc
283	        elif not isinstance(loc, Loc):
284	            loc = self.get_loc(loc)
285	        return TileSyntaxError(message, loc)
286	
287	    def unsupported_syntax(self, loc=None) -> TileSyntaxError:
288	        return self.syntax_error("Unsupported syntax", loc=loc)
